tossups.question: While tutoring a girl who adores her pekinese Looloo, a character in this novel is horrified by the strength of the rabbit Bismarck. David Lodge’s entry on symbolism in The Art of Fiction is a scene in this novel in which a man forces a terrified mare to stand by a railway crossing. In this novel, friends of the lisping model Pussum mockingly read a letter about the “Flux of Corruption” at a London café, leading the writer’s sister-in-law to grab the letter and walk out with it. This novel is the (*) sequel to a novel that ends with a vision of the “old, brittle corruption of houses and factories” being swept away by the title image in the sky. On a climactic trip to the Alps in this novel, the sculptor Loerke is attacked in the snow by the industrialist Gerald Crich, the intimate “man friend” of Rupert Birkin. For 10 points, name this novel about Gudrun and Ursula Brangwen, a sequel to The Rainbow by D. H. Lawrence.

In this novel, friends of the lisping model Pussum mockingly read a letter about the "Flux of Corruption" at a London cafe, leading the writer's sister-in-law to grab the letter and walk out with it. This novel is the (*) sequel to a novel that ends with a vision of the "old, brittle corruption of houses and factories" being swept away by the title image in the sky. On a climactic trip to the Alps in this novel, the sculptor Loerke is attacked in the snow by the industrialist Gerald Crich, the intimate "man friend" of Rupert Birkin. For 10 points, name this novel about Gudrun and Ursula Brangwen, a sequel to The Rainbow by D. H. Lawrence.

bonuses.leadin: Elizabeth finds out that her husband Walter died in a coalmine accident in this author’s story “Odour of Chrysanthemums.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author who wrote about a boy who can clairvoyantly predict the outcome of horse races in “The Rocking-Horse Winner”
Constance is the title character of this Lawrence novel, in which she has an affair with gamekeeper Oliver Mellors. It was the subject of some notable obscenity trials.
This Lawrence novel follows the romantic relationships of Rupert Birkin and Gerald Crich with the Brangwen sisters. He previously wrote about the Brangwen family in The Rainbow.
